title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a keyed order and failover state machine in Go, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a keyed order and failover state machine in Go while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ordering Scope Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 7893 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ordering Scope Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ordering Scope Contract, keep Ordering Scope Contract connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Ordering Throughput Tradeoff

workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 6274 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
